exercises.description: Order the sentences (B-G) below to make a story. {v} The first sentence of the story (A) is given for you as an example. {v} A.-The Incas were an ancient population that lived in South America.
exercises.label: Reading Part 2

questions.title: B Cuzco in Peru was the capital of the Inca Empire. They were fine architects and constructed places such as Machu Picchu.
C  This Empire was started by Viracocha, who was the supreme God of the Incas.
D The prime Inca structures were made of stone blocks. These constructions have survived for centuries.
E The Incas were defeated by the Spanish in 1533, as their capital Cuzco was captured.
F They were part of the Inca Empire, which started in 1425 A.D.
G The Spanish conquistador Francisco Pizarro and his army attacked the Incas in 1532.

exercises.description: The sentences below are from some instructions, Put these sentences in the right order. The first sentences are done for you {v} A. If you are going to apply for a University, please follow these instructions.

questions.title: B Then wait for the University to inform you if they accepted the application.
C Decide which university you want to go and check the qualifications that you need.
D Next Submit your application.
E You should decide what you want to study and find the best universities for that career.
F Fill in the qualifications.

exercises.heading: Learning Languages
exercises.description: Person A {v} Nowadays, there are countless online platforms that allow for students to practice a new language with native speakers of that language. It’s one thing to study a textbook, but online connections allow us to take that information we have learned and put it into practice through a real-life conversation. What is the point of learning a language if you don’t know how to communicate properly with it? {v} Person B {v} They say that you only truly grasp a language when you can dream in it or make jokes in it. I get that, but too many people these days are so focused on what they can produce that they forget about what they can understand. Reaching an advanced level won’t happen if every time you talk to a foreigner it’s a one-sided conversation in which you don’t understand anyone but yourself. {v} Person C {v} One of the things I struggled with in foreign language classes at school was not the grammar or the writing - I had no problem understanding grammar rules and memorizing vocabulary - but being understood when I spoke. I only got a bit better at pronunciation when I made friends online who were patient enough to help me focus on my problems. Nevertheless, I still struggle with it and highly recommend teachers putting more emphasis on it. {v} Person D {v} My degree was in translation so, as one can imagine, I spent hours upon hours studying grammar, watching movies and reading in my target languages. Without a doubt, if I hadn’t invested so much time in the structure of a language and its conjugations, I would not have been as successful in my career. Speaking came easy to me because I had confidence that my sentences were well built and correct.
exercises.subtitle: Four people respond to a blog post about learning new languages. Read their comments and answer the questions below.
exercises.label: Reading Part 4

questions.title: 1. Who thinks that online resources can be useful, but are not the solution to problems in language learning?
2. Who thinks that online learning is an amazing resource?
3. Who thinks that not enough focus is put on listening in language learning?
4. Who thinks that more focus needs to be put on pronunciation in language learning?
5. Who thinks that the base of language learning is the grammar?
6. Who dedicated a lot of time to learning new languages?
7. Who thinks that mastering a language has to do with being able to speak it?

exercises.description: Read the text below. Match the headings A-H to the paragraphs 19-25.
exercises.label: Reading Part 5

answers: C. What inspired Doyle to create Sherlock Holmes?
D. Family history of Sherlock and Doyle
questions.title: 19. Sherlock Holmes is a fictional private detective character, created by the British author, {v}  Sir Arthur Conan Doyle. The character of C. Auguste Dupin in Edgar Allan Poe's {v}  “The Murders in the Rue Morgue” is said to be the primary inspiration behind Holmes. {v} Conan Doyle also mentioned a real person by the name of Joseph Bell as an {v} inspiration behind the character of Sherlock Holmes. Bell was a surgeon at the Royal {v} Infirmary of Edinburgh, who Conan Doyle had met while attending medical school in {v} 1877. Apart from Bell, another real person named Sir Henry Littlejohn was also {v} considered as a direct inspiration. He was a Police Surgeon and Medical Officer in {v} Edinburgh. The link between Holmes’ abilities as a detective and a medical investigator {v} is clear.

answers: F. Biographies of the fictional characters
questions.title: 20. According to references found in Doyle’s books, Holmes was born some time during {v} 1854. There is no information regarding the parents of Holmes, although he himself {v} described them as ‘country squires.’ Holmes also claimed that his grandmother was the {v} sister of French artist Vernet. He had a brother named Mycroft, who was seven years {v} older than him. Mycroft appeared in a handful of Sherlock Holmes adventures. {v} Holmes started detective work as early as his university days. Due to financial {v} problems, he had to accept Watson as a lodge-mate in 1881. They stayed together at {v} 221B Baker Street, London. Holmes did detective work for 23 years, 17 of those with {v} Watson. The character of Holmes was killed in 1893 in “The Final Problem,” but {v} reappeared in another adventure some years later.

answers: A. Education and skills of Sherlock Holmes
questions.title: 21. The educational background of Sherlock Holmes is explained within the pages of the {v} first novel ‘A study in Scarlet.’ He was a student of Chemistry in 1881, whose interests {v} made him good at solving crimes. Watson assessed Holmes’ abilities and skills in the {v} first novel. He deduced that Holmes had significant knowledge of Chemistry and {v} anatomy, was a good violin player, and knew a lot about British law and sensational {v} literature. Sherlock Holmes was also an expert sportsman, and quite good as a {v} swordsman, boxer, and singlestick player. Holmes also knew Latin, had an extensive {v} knowledge of art, could decipher many forms of secret writing, and was an expert in {v} studying physical evidence.

22. The 56 Sherlock Holmes stories and 4 novels are known as the ‘canon’ by fans of the {v} series. Christopher Morley of New York had formed the first society dedicated to {v} Sherlock Holmes’ legacy, named the Baker Street Irregulars. Ronald Knox created a {v} game known as the Sherlockian Game. The Sherlock Holmes Society in London was {v} formed in 1934, and the Sherlock Holmes museum was opened at Baker Street in 1990. {v} Both still exist today. Many other societies were also established to pay tribute to the {v} legacy of Holmes. Currently, there are more than 250 Sherlock Holmes societies {v} spread across the world. One of the locomotives of the London Metropolitan Railway {v} was also named after him.

answers: B. Early adaptations and derived works from the Sherlock Holmes series
questions.title: 23. There are many examples of art, literature and audiovisual media that are adaptations, {v} based on Sherlock Holmes. The very first recognisable pastiche (visual art piece) of {v} Holmes, dates back to 1893. In the animated series ‘Sherlock Holmes in the 22nd {v} Century’, Sherlock is brought back from the dead to fight future villains. Nicholas {v} Meyer's ‘The Seven-Per-Cent Solution’ is a 1974 novel based on Holmes, which was {v} also made into a film in 1976. There have been many screen adaptations of Sherlock {v} Holmes, since the 1900 film ‘Sherlock Holmes Baffled’.

answers: E. Modern takes on the classic
questions.title: 24.Among the most well-known 21st century screen adaptations is the film series by {v} director Guy Ritchie, starring Robert Downey Jr. and Jude Law, released in 2009 and {v} 2011, with a third installment expected to be released in 2021. The classic has also {v} been given a modern twist, being reimagined in the 21st century by television series like {v} “Sherlock,” starring Benedict Cumberbatch and Martin Freeman, perhaps the most {v} popular of the modern adaptations, and “Elementary,” starring Jonny Lee Miller as {v} Holmes and Lucy Liu as Watson.

answers: G. Other works left to us by Doyle
questions.title: 25. Conan Doyle himself wrote many different short stories that were not part of the {v} Sherlock Holmes series, and in fact, predated these most famous works. Some {v} examples of these stories include “The Mystery of Sasassa Valley,” “The American {v} Tale,” and “Captain of the Pole Star.” Doyle also wrote plays based on Holmes. Many of {v} his later writings, however, such as “The New Revolution,” and “The Wanderings of a {v} Spiritualist,” were focused less on fictional characters and more on his religious {v} interest in what Doyle referred to as “Spiritualism.” Doyle eventually died of a heart {v} attack in 1930, leaving behind his imaginative legacy for generations to come.

questions.title: Question 1: What does the headmaster think about school uniforms in general? {x}
answers: They tend to limit the free expression and individuality of each student.
They are more effective in less privileged schools as they give students a sense of pride.
They allow students to focus more on academics and therefore, do better in school.

questions.title: Question 2: How does the headmaster think school uniforms affect students' feelings towards their school?  {x}
answers: It causes students to develop negative feelings as they feel they must conform to certain rules.
It allows for students to feel a sense of unity with their school and classmates.
It has neither a positive or negative impact on how students feel.
